name: Shaky Isles North
description: A tough North Island route with remote stretches and mixed surfaces.
difficultyExplanation: Shaky Isles North has rougher lines, bigger climbing days, and more remote riding than easier touring routes. Longer gaps between dependable services push it into hard territory.
beAwareOf: Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress.
tips: Start early, carry a weather margin, and keep backup navigation plus an extra food and water buffer.
trickiestSection: The Timber Trail and Mokau headwaters combination is the most remote two-day block, requiring good weather judgement and full food carry.
routeSummary: Shaky Isles North is a hard bikepacking route covering about 1,601 km, commonly ridden over 9 days. A tough North Island route with remote stretches and mixed surfaces. Riders can expect 45% Gravel / 15% Road / 40% Track as the route unfolds.

Good planning makes this route far more enjoyable. The best riding window is generally Oct-Apr, with resupply described as Carry enough food and water for longer gaps between settlements; verify opening hours before committing to remote sections.. Overnight options are typically A mix of camping, huts, and local stays depending on your pace and daily target distance..

The key section to manage is: The Timber Trail and Mokau headwaters combination is the most remote two-day block, requiring good weather judgement and full food carry. Keep a close eye on Weather changes, wind exposure, and changing trail conditions can all materially affect daily progress., especially if conditions change during the day.
recommendedSetupText: For this 9-day route, we recommend a Hardtail MTB to balance comfort and control across mixed terrain. This setup keeps key gear stable and easy to access over long days.
